children.name: Mia
children.reading_goal: Read short stories with smoother fluency and stronger comprehension

stories.title: Mia and the Moon Cat
stories.story_text: Mia loved to draw pictures of space. One night, she drew a big yellow moon and a fluffy cat sitting on it. "I wish I could visit the moon," Mia said. Suddenly, her drawing started to glow. The moon cat jumped out of the paper and meowed softly. "Hello, Mia," the cat said. "I'm Luna. Hop on my back, and let's fly to the moon!" Mia held tight as Luna soared through the stars. When they landed, Mia saw craters and sparkling rocks. Luna explained, "Craters are big holes made by meteorites. They're like giant footprints!" Mia laughed and drew a picture of the craters. Then they found a shiny crystal and a tiny moon rabbit. Mia felt proud because she used her imagination and noticed details. When it was time to go home, Luna gave Mia a star as a gift. "Keep drawing, Mia. Your imagination can take you anywhere!" Mia smiled and hugged her new friend.

vocabulary.meaning: a big bowl-shaped hole on the moon
rocks from space that land on a moon or planet
the power to picture new ideas in your mind

questions.question: What did Mia draw one night?
options: A big yellow moon and a fluffy cat
A red boat and a fish
A green tree and a bird
questions.answer: A big yellow moon and a fluffy cat
questions.explanation: Mia drew a big yellow moon with a fluffy cat sitting on it.

questions.question: What happened after the moon cat jumped out of the paper?
options: Mia rode Luna to the moon
questions.answer: Mia rode Luna to the moon
questions.explanation: After Luna jumped out, Mia hopped on her back and flew to the moon.

questions.question: What does crater mean in the story?
options: A big hole on the moon
questions.answer: A big hole on the moon
questions.explanation: Luna explains that craters are big holes made by meteorites.

questions.question: Why did Mia feel proud on the moon?
options: She used her imagination and noticed details
questions.answer: She used her imagination and noticed details
questions.explanation: Mia felt proud because she imagined the adventure and paid attention to what she saw.

answers.3: She used her imagination and noticed details

children.name: Leo
children.reading_goal: Build vocabulary confidence and explain story clues with evidence

stories.title: Leo and the Robot Goalkeeper
stories.story_text: Leo carried his soccer ball to the quiet field behind the library. Near the goal, he found a small robot with one blinking blue eye. The robot held up a card that said, "Can you solve my training mystery?" Leo grinned and read the card aloud. The first clue was on the net: "Notice the pattern." Leo saw three red marks, then three blue marks, then three red marks again. He aimed at the next blue mark and scored. The robot spun in a happy circle. The second clue was hidden under a cone. It said, "Explain your evidence." Leo said, "The colors repeat, so blue comes next." The robot opened its metal hands and showed Leo a golden whistle. "You used details like a detective," it beeped. Leo practiced one more shot, then wrote the pattern in his notebook so he could remember it tomorrow.

vocabulary.meaning: the player who protects the goal
something that needs to be solved
something that repeats in a clear way
details that help prove an answer

vocabulary.hint: Look for the long e sound

questions.question: Where did Leo find the robot?
questions.explanation: Leo found the robot near the goal on the quiet field.

questions.question: What did Leo do after seeing the color pattern?
options: He aimed at the next blue mark
questions.answer: He aimed at the next blue mark
questions.explanation: Leo noticed the repeating colors and aimed at the next blue mark.

questions.question: What does evidence mean in the story?
questions.explanation: Evidence means details that help prove an answer.

questions.question: Why did the robot give Leo the golden whistle?
options: Leo used details to solve the clues
questions.answer: Leo used details to solve the clues
questions.explanation: The robot rewarded Leo because he explained the pattern with evidence.

answers.1: He aimed at the next blue mark
answers.3: Leo used details to solve the clues
